preview_text:
The model favors South Korea at 47%, with Czech Republic assigned a 23% chance of victory. That 24-percentage-point gap reflects a clear home-field or competitive edge in the forecast, though the underdog retains meaningful odds.

The draw sits at 31% in the model, suggesting a reasonably balanced contest. The win probabilities create a spread where South Korea is the clear lean but neither team's outright loss is heavily discounted. A stalemate emerges as a plausible outcome, indicating this is unlikely to be a runaway performance.

A substantial divergence appears between the model and the sportsbooks. The books compress South Korea's win probability to 36%—roughly 11 percentage points lower than the model—while elevating Czech Republic to 33%. Polymarket prices align closely with the books on this gap. The model and market assign similar odds to the draw. No consensus exists yet on which assessment is more accurate; the dashboard presents both without claiming superiority for either.
